strInstructions: Mix ground beef with cayenne, black pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, salt, paprika, and oregano until sticky. Shape into a loaf and bake until cooked through. Chill, slice thinly, and pan-fry the slices. Warm pita bread, fill with donair meat, tomato, onion, and sweet garlic donair sauce.

Divide leftover bread dough into small balls and flatten into rounds. Let the rounds rest briefly on a floured surface. Heat oil in a skillet and fry the dough slowly until golden on both sides and cooked through. Serve warm with butter and molasses.

Soak salted cod and hard tack overnight. Simmer sliced potatoes and soaked hard tack until tender. Boil the salt cod separately until flaky. Render diced salt pork until crisp, then sauté onions in the fat. Combine the potatoes, hard tack, cod, onions, and scrunchions, then finish under the broiler if desired.

Place cod fillets in a greased baking dish. Make a white sauce with butter, flour, and scalded milk, then season with Dijon mustard, lemon zest, savoury, salt, and pepper. Stir in Parmesan and pour over the fish. Top with cheddar and cracker crumbs mixed with olive oil. Bake until bubbling and browned.

Prepare pizza dough and stretch it into rounds. Mix softened butter with minced garlic and garlic powder, then spread over the dough. Top with shredded mozzarella. Bake until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling. Slice into fingers and serve with donair sauce.

Slice beef into thin strips and coat with cornstarch batter. Fry until crisp. Stir-fry carrots, peppers, ginger, garlic, and chili flakes, then add a sweet vinegar soy sauce. Toss the crispy beef in the sauce and serve hot with rice.

In a mixing bowl, whisk evaporated milk, white vinegar, garlic powder, white sugar, and salt until smooth and slightly thickened. Cover the bowl and refrigerate until chilled. Stir again before serving as a sweet garlic sauce for Halifax donair, garlic fingers, or fries.

Combine rolled oats, coconut, and cocoa powder in a large bowl. In a saucepan, melt butter with evaporated milk and sugar, then bring to a gentle boil. Pour the hot mixture over the oat mixture and stir until fully combined. Let cool slightly, then roll into balls. Coat each ball with extra coconut and chill until firm before serving.

Mix melted butter, brown sugar, flour, cocoa powder, vanilla, and egg until combined. Spread the base mixture into a prepared baking pan. Bake until set, then cool slightly. Add the coconut topping and chocolate layer according to the recipe style, then chill until firm before cutting into squares.

In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, and baking powder. Cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in the raisins, then add milk gradually until a soft dough forms. Shape into buns and place on a baking sheet. Bake until golden brown and cooked through. Serve warm with butter.

Pat the cod fillets dry. Set up separate bowls with flour, beaten eggs, and bread crumbs. Coat each fillet in flour, dip in egg, then cover with bread crumbs. Heat butter and canola oil in a skillet over medium heat. Pan-fry the cod until golden brown on both sides and cooked through. Serve hot with lemon if desired.

Chop the cooked shrimp and combine with egg, minced onion, bread crumbs, sea salt, and black pepper. Mix until the mixture holds together, then shape into burger patties. Cook the patties in a skillet until golden and heated through. Serve on buns with preferred toppings.

Combine cooked lobster meat, mayonnaise, celery, and lemon juice in a bowl. Mix gently so the lobster stays chunky. Butter the hot dog buns and toast them until golden. Fill each bun with the lobster mixture and serve immediately.

Heat a skillet over medium heat and cook the peameal bacon slices for 3 to 4 minutes per side until browned and fully heated through. Lightly butter the Kaiser rolls and toast them until warm. Layer the cooked peameal bacon onto the rolls and top with yellow mustard if desired. Serve immediately while hot.

Mix flour, yeast, warm water, sugar, and salt into a dough. Knead until smooth, then let rise until doubled. Divide the dough and shape into bagels. Boil the bagels briefly in water, then coat with sesame seeds. Bake until golden brown and firm.

Preheat the oven according to the pizza crust directions. Spread pizza sauce over the crust, then top with mozzarella cheese, cooked ham, and drained pineapple chunks. Drizzle lightly with olive oil if desired. Bake until the cheese is melted and the crust is golden. Slice and serve hot.

Preheat the oven according to the pizza dough instructions. Roll out the pizza dough onto a lightly floured surface and transfer it to a pizza pan or baking sheet. Spread the pizza sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a small border around the edges. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese over the sauce, then top with Canadian bacon slices and red onion. Bake until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly. Remove from the oven, garnish with parsley if desired, slice, and serve hot.

Whisk buckwheat flour, all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt together in a bowl. Stir in cold water, then add boiling water and mix until a thin batter forms. Let the batter rest briefly. Heat a dry skillet or griddle over medium heat, then pour in a thin layer of batter. Cook on one side only until the surface dries and bubbles form. Serve warm with butter, molasses, maple syrup, or savory toppings.

Place the chicken pieces in a large pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil, skim off any foam, then add onion, celery, carrots, potatoes, savory, salt, and black pepper. Reduce the heat and simmer until the chicken is tender and the vegetables are cooked. Prepare dumplings by mixing flour, baking powder, salt, and water into a soft dough. Drop spoonfuls of dough into the simmering soup, cover, and cook until the dumplings are firm and cooked through. Serve hot.

Preheat the oven to 350°F. In a large bowl, combine the beef, pork, veal, onion, potatoes, salt, pepper, cinnamon, cloves, and savory. Line a deep baking dish with pie pastry. Layer the meat and potato mixture into the dish, then pour beef broth over the filling. Cover with another pastry sheet and seal the edges. Cut small vents on top and bake until the crust is golden brown and the filling is fully cooked and tender.

Rinse the split peas under cold water. In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté the onion, carrots, and celery until softened. Add garlic and cook briefly. Stir in the split peas, ham bone, chicken broth, water, bay leaves, thyme, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer until the peas are soft and the soup is thick. Remove the ham bone, shred any meat, and return it to the soup before serving.

Preheat the oven to 350°F or 175°C. In a saucepan, bring maple syrup, heavy cream, and salt to a gentle boil, then remove from heat. In a bowl, whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. In another bowl, cream butter and sugar, then beat in the eggs, milk, and vanilla. Stir in the dry ingredients to form a batter. Spread the batter into a baking dish and carefully pour the hot maple cream sauce over the top without stirring. Bake until the cake is golden and the maple sauce is bubbling underneath. Serve warm.

Place the chicken pieces into a large pot, cover with water, and simmer until fully cooked and tender. Remove the chicken, reserve the broth, and shred the meat. Grate the potatoes finely and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. Slowly pour the hot reserved broth into the grated potatoes until the mixture softens and thickens slightly. Spread part of the potato mixture into a greased baking dish, layer with shredded chicken, then cover with the remaining potato mixture. Dot with butter and bake until the top is golden brown and the pie is cooked through.

Combine blueberries, sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, and water in a large skillet or saucepan over medium heat. Simmer until the berries release their juices and the mixture becomes bubbly. In a bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt. Cut in the butter, then stir in the milk until a soft dough forms. Drop spoonfuls of dough over the blueberry mixture, cover tightly, and steam gently until the dumplings are puffed and cooked through. Serve warm.

Steam the Montreal smoked meat until hot and tender. Warm the rye bread lightly. Spread yellow mustard on the bread, pile the smoked meat high, and close the sandwich. Slice in half and serve with pickles.

Preheat the oven to 350°F or 175°C and grease a 9x13-inch baking pan. For the filling, simmer chopped dates, butter, sugar, vanilla, and boiling water until thick, then set aside to cool slightly. In a large bowl, mix rolled oats, baking powder, coconut, flour, and brown sugar. Rub in the butter until the mixture forms coarse crumbs. Press half of the crumble mixture into the prepared pan, spread the date filling over it, and scatter the remaining crumble mixture on top. Bake until golden and set, then cool before slicing into squares.
strMeasure2: 3 tbsp for filling plus 1 1/2 cups for crumble

strInstructions: Pack clean snow or finely crushed ice into a large tray. Pour maple syrup into a heavy-bottomed saucepan and bring it to a boil over high heat. Boil until the syrup reaches 240°F or 115°C on a candy thermometer. Remove the pan from the heat. With a ladle, pour thin lines of hot maple taffy over the snow. Let it cool briefly, then roll the taffy around wooden sticks and serve immediately.

Combine Saskatoon berries, sugar, lemon juice, and flour in a bowl. Line a pie plate with pastry and fill with the berry mixture. Dot with butter, cover with a second pastry crust, and seal the edges. Cut vents in the top crust and bake until golden brown and bubbling.

Prepare the graham cracker crust and press it into a pie plate. Cook the custard filling until thickened, then pour it into the crust. Beat the egg whites into a meringue and spread it over the custard. Bake until the meringue is golden, then cool before slicing.
strMeasure2: 1/2 cup for crust plus 1/2 cup for filling

strInstructions: Soak the salt beef overnight, then drain. Place the beef in a large pot with water and simmer until tender. Add cabbage, potatoes, carrots, turnip, and parsnips during the final hour of cooking. Serve the vegetables and beef hot with mustard pickles if desired.

Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, raisins, and cinnamon in a bowl. Cut in butter, then stir in milk to form a thick dough. Place the dough in a greased pudding mold or cloth, seal tightly, and boil or steam until firm. Slice and serve warm with molasses or rum sauce.

Boil potatoes until tender, then mash with butter and milk. Brown the ground beef with onion, salt, and pepper. Spread the beef in a baking dish, top with creamed corn and whole-kernel corn, then cover with mashed potatoes. Bake until the top is lightly golden.

Combine ground pork, onion, garlic, milk, cloves, cinnamon, allspice, salt, and pepper in a saucepan. Simmer slowly, stirring often, until the pork is very tender and the mixture becomes spreadable. Mash slightly, chill until firm, and serve on toast.

Preheat the oven to 375°F or 190°C. In a large bowl, combine rolled oats, flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt. Cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add hot water gradually and mix until a firm dough forms. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface and cut into rectangles or rounds. Place on a baking sheet and bake until the oatcakes are lightly golden and crisp at the edges. Cool before serving.

Mix ground pork with onion, breadcrumbs, egg, cinnamon, cloves, salt, and pepper. Shape into meatballs and brown in a pot. Add broth and simmer until the meatballs are cooked through. Thicken the sauce with toasted flour and serve hot with potatoes.

Slice the salted pork fat into thin strips. Soak briefly in water if very salty, then pat dry. Fry the pork strips slowly until the fat renders and the pieces become crisp and golden. Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

Whisk brown sugar, cream, eggs, flour, vanilla, and salt until smooth. Pour the filling into an unbaked pie shell. Bake until the crust is golden and the filling is set but slightly soft in the center. Cool before slicing.

Roll out pie dough into a rectangle. Spread softened butter over the dough, then sprinkle with brown sugar and cinnamon. Roll tightly into a log, slice into rounds, and place in a baking dish. Bake until golden and caramelized.

Cut salmon into strips and marinate with maple syrup, brown sugar, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper. Refrigerate until the salmon absorbs the marinade. Smoke the salmon over low heat until firm, glossy, and slightly chewy, brushing with extra maple syrup near the end.

Soak the dried navy beans overnight, then drain. Place the beans in a large pot, cover with water, and simmer until tender. Transfer the beans to a baking dish or bean pot. Stir in diced bacon or salt pork, onion, maple syrup, brown sugar, molasses, dry mustard, salt, and pepper. Cover and bake slowly until the beans are tender, dark, and thickened.

Place the chopped dates in a bowl with boiling water and baking soda, then let stand until softened. Cream the butter and sugar, then beat in the egg and vanilla. Stir in the date mixture, flour, baking powder, and salt until combined. Spread the batter in a baking pan and bake until the cake is set. For the topping, melt butter with brown sugar and cream, stir in coconut, spread over the warm cake, and broil briefly until bubbling and golden.
strMeasure4: 1/4 cup for cake plus 1/4 cup for topping

strInstructions: Simmer raisins, water, brown sugar, shortening, cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg, and salt until the raisins are plump and the sugar dissolves. Let the mixture cool. Stir in flour and baking soda until a thick batter forms. Spread into a greased loaf pan and bake until firm and fragrant. Cool before slicing.

Combine cubed beef, pork, veal, potatoes, onion, salt, pepper, cinnamon, and cloves in a large bowl and refrigerate overnight. Line a deep baking dish with pastry dough and fill with the meat and potato mixture. Pour beef broth over the filling, cover with another pastry crust, and seal the edges. Cut vents on top and bake slowly until the crust is golden brown and the meat is tender.

Prepare a sweet yeast dough with milk, yeast, sugar, eggs, butter, flour, cinnamon, and salt. Let the dough rise until doubled, then roll, cut, and shape into spiral rolls. Fry the rolls in hot oil until golden brown. Beat icing sugar with raspberry jam and milk to make the pink icing, then spread over the cooled rolls.

Wash the fiddleheads thoroughly and boil them briefly before draining. Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion until softened. Stir in the flour and cook briefly, then gradually whisk in the chicken broth. Add potatoes and simmer until tender. Stir in the fiddleheads and cook until tender. Add the cream, season with salt and pepper, and blend partially if desired for a smoother texture. Serve hot.

Mix flour, baking powder, salt, and lard in a large bowl until crumbly. Gradually stir in water to form a soft dough. Knead lightly on a floured surface, then shape into rounds or flatten into discs. Cook in a skillet over medium heat until golden brown on both sides or bake until cooked through. Serve warm with butter, jam, or savory toppings.

Soak the cedar plank in water before cooking. Place the salmon on the soaked plank and brush with maple syrup, mustard, oil, salt, and pepper. Grill or bake until the salmon flakes easily and has absorbed the cedar aroma. Rest briefly before serving.

Prepare a soft yeast dough and let it rise until doubled. Roll out the dough and spread with butter, brown sugar, cinnamon, and raisins. Roll tightly and slice into buns. Place in a pan with maple syrup and butter, then bake until golden and sticky. Turn out while warm so the syrup coats the buns.

Whisk mayonnaise and honey together in a bowl until smooth. Crush the dried dill lightly in your hand to release its aroma, then stir it into the sauce. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour so the flavours blend. Serve chilled as a dip for chicken fingers, fries, or vegetables.

Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and blueberries in a bowl. Stir in melted butter, milk, and vanilla to form a thick batter. Spoon the batter into a greased pudding mold or cloth. Steam until firm and cooked through. Serve warm with blueberry sauce or rum sauce.

Stir graham crumbs, coconut, chocolate chips, and sweetened condensed milk together until sticky. Chill briefly if needed, then roll into small balls. Roll each ball in extra graham crumbs or coconut until coated. Refrigerate until firm before serving.

Cream butter and sugar, then mix in flour to make a soft cookie base. Press into a baking pan and partially bake. Spread partridgeberry jam over the base. Beat egg whites with sugar until glossy, fold in coconut, and spread over the jam. Bake until the meringue is golden and set. Cool before cutting into squares.
strMeasure2: 1/2 cup for base plus 1 cup for meringue

strInstructions: Cream butter and sugar until light, then beat in eggs and vanilla. Stir in flour, baking powder, and salt to make a soft dough. Roll and cut the dough into rounds. Bake until lightly golden, then sandwich pairs of cookies with raspberry jam.

Mix flour, baking soda, spices, raisins, and salt in a bowl. Stir molasses, milk, and melted butter into the dry ingredients to make a thick batter. Spoon into a greased pudding mold, cover tightly, and steam until set. Serve warm with butterscotch sauce.

Mix flour, butter, sugar, and vanilla into a crumbly cookie base. Press into a baking pan and bake lightly. Spread red berry jam over the base. Combine coconut, sugar, eggs, and vanilla, then spread over the jam. Bake until golden, cool completely, and cut into squares.
strMeasure3: 1/4 cup for base plus 1 cup for topping

strInstructions: Cook crushed pineapple with sugar and cornstarch until thickened, then cool. Prepare a crumb base with flour, butter, sugar, and vanilla. Press part of the crumbs into a pan, spread the pineapple filling over it, then top with remaining crumbs. Chill or bake according to texture preference before slicing into squares.

Cream butter and brown sugar, then beat in molasses and egg. Stir in flour, baking soda, spices, raisins, and pecans. Drop spoonfuls of dough onto baking sheets and bake until soft and set. Cool before serving.

Mix graham crumbs with melted butter and press into a springform pan. Beat cream cheese, sugar, eggs, vanilla, and sour cream until smooth. Pour over the crust and bake until just set. Chill completely, then spoon bakeapple jam over the top before serving.

Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt together. Cream butter and sugar, then add milk and the dry ingredients to make a batter. Spread in a baking dish and sprinkle with walnuts. Heat maple syrup, brown sugar, cream, and water until dissolved, then pour over the batter. Bake until the cake rises and the maple sauce bubbles underneath.

Prepare a shortbread base by mixing butter, flour, and brown sugar, then press into a pan and bake until set. Cook bacon until crisp and chop it. Pour maple filling over the crust, sprinkle with bacon, and bake until the filling is set. Cool completely before cutting into bars.
